title: Gossampinus thorelii
taxonomy: Gossampinus thorelii is a species within the flowering plant family Lamiaceae. It is a genus of flowering plants that belongs to the family Oleaceae. Gossampinus thorelii is a part of the order Lamiales. The family Lamiaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as the mint family. Gossampinus thorelii, along with other members of the family, exhibits characteristics such as opposite or whorled leaves and inflorescences that are often tubular in shape.
uses: Gossampinus thorelii is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. Its bark and leaves are said to possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
